```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  fx <- sample(seq(0.10,0.90,0.05),1); x <- sample(-2:5,1)
  vals <- round(c(1-fx,fx,1/(1+fx),fx*(1-fx),1-fx^2),3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Para certo $x=`r x`$, sabe-se que $F_X(x)=`r fx`$. Qual é $P(X>x)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Como $F_X(x)=P(X\leq x)$,
$$P(X>x)=1-F_X(x)=1-`r fx`=`r sprintf("%.3f",1-fx)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S5Q10_funcao_sobrevivencia
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
